summary: Drag'n'drop of a PDF (from file explorer) to group should add the file

description.text:
1. Open Windows Explorer
2. Navigate into folder `main/src/test/resources/pdfs/PdfContentImporter`
3. Open JabRef
4. Create new library
5. Save library to folder `main/src/test/resources/pdfs/PdfContentImporter`
6. In the group view on the left: Click on "Add group" 
7. Enter "test" as name
8. Click on "OK"
9. If no group is added, repeat steps 5 to 7 (another issue in JabRef)
10. Drag `Baldoni2002.pdf` onto JabRef table
11. Result: PDF added
12. Drag `Bogner2019.pdf` onto group "test"
13. After dropping: nothing happens

Expected behavior:

1. PDF is imported as in step 10
2. PDF is added to group test

Hint: Method `org.jabref.gui.maintable.MainTable#handleOnDragDroppedTableView` is handling the drop on the main table.

summary: Fix Citationkey pattern dropdown UI issue

description.text:
### Follow up for #12516

Issue: The added dropdown has an issue, it shifts upwards when it reaches the bottom of the screen due to the default behavior of Context Menu in JavaFX.

This PR ensures that the dropdown is properly positioned and dynamically adjusts its height based on the available space. 

Additionally, the "All patterns" submenu is always shown when displaying the suggestions list is not possible, ensuring that the  UX is not compromised.

summary: User-defined keyword separator does not apply to Merge Groups

description.text:
### JabRef version

Latest development branch build (please note build date below)

### Operating system

GNU / Linux

### Details on version and operating system

openSUSE Tumbleweed

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

The user-defined "Keyword separator" applies to keywords and groups in the entry table, but not in the Merge/Find duplicates window. The correct separator is used to merge keywords, but not groups.
 
1. Set a non-default keyword separator in Preferences > Entry
2. Notice that the keyword separator also applies to the `groups` field (as intended)
3. Create two entries with at least one group per entry but not identical group sets.
4. Select the two entries and click Quality > Merge Entries
6. In the merge window, Click the icon for `Merge Groups`
7. Notice that the groups are joined using the default separator (comma) instead of the user-defined character.

The keyword separator is applies to `keywords` and `groups`, so I expect the same separator to be used for merging keywords or merging groups.

### Appendix

JabRef 100.0.0
Linux 6.12.8-2-default amd64 
Java 23.0.1 
JavaFX 23.0.2+3
...
<details>
<summary>Log File</summary>

```
Not applicable
```

</details>

### JabRef version

6.0-alpha

### Operating system

Windows

### Details on version and operating system

Windows 11 24H2

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

1. Add `pagetotal` to entry table columns.
2. Add entries with varying `pagetotal`s.
3. Sort: ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/15b49695-4b9a-49ee-b527-e044fbdb7bee)


### Appendix

_No response_

summary: custom terminal not predictably interacting with terminal

description.text:
### JabRef version

Latest development branch build (please note build date below)

### Operating system

GNU / Linux

### Details on version and operating system

JabRef 6.0-alpha.43--2025-01-20--4aa313d Linux 6.12.12-200.fc41.x86_64 amd64  Java 23.0.1  JavaFX 23.0.1+4

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

I wonder whether the issue relates to wayland, gnome or fedora linux..., based on our discussion at the [forum](https://discourse.jabref.org/t/external-programs-custom-terminal/5418/2).


2. in the JabRef settings, specify a custom terminal call ` /usr/bin/gnome-terminal --working-directory='%DIR'`, accept
3. back in the main window, use Menu tools/open terminal here.

In my case, JabRef seems to resolve `'%DIR'` correctly (I can copy the event log's command and paste it in the terminal manually, and there the call is effective), but no terminal appears on my gnome desktop, neither in the background nor in the foreground.

The quotes `'...'` are necessary for my terminal to handle the directory path, which includes blanks...

### Appendix

_No response_

summary: When opening JabRef, Ctrl+W does not behave as expected.

description.text:
### JabRef version

5.15 (latest release)

### Operating system

Windows

### Details on version and operating system

_No response_

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

There is a bug in the Ctrl+W shortcut. Instead of closing the current tab, it always closes the first tab in JabRef.

1. Open JabRef.
2. Use Ctrl+W key.
3. It will close the first tab, instead of current tab.

summary: Preferences: Add manual prompt control for cross-references in Copy To

description.text:
This adds an option to "General->User Interface" to manually enable/disable the prompt that asks the user if they would like to include cross-references when using "Copy to". 

This is primarily to allow users to re-enable the prompts if they have disabled them from within the prompt.

Closes #12501

![Screenshot 2025-02-17 182257](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1eff4a5d-63b6-40fe-855d-9be541c24d7c)

summary: Fix #11799: Resolved file renaming dialog issue and made it resizable

description.text:
This pull request resolves #11799 by:

- Setting a preferred size for the file renaming dialog (500x200) to accommodate long file names and improve usability.
- Adding setResizable(true) to allow users to resize the dialog, providing greater flexibility across different screen sizes and resolutions.

summary: Fix linked files tooltip to display all files on hover

description.text:
Fixes #12470 

this PR fixes hovering over the Linked Files tab, the tooltip now displays all the files linked

Updated getDescriptionAndLink() to return all linked files' descriptions and links.

Previously, the tooltip was set using .withStringTooltip(LinkedFileViewModel::getDescriptionAndLink), which only fetched one file.

updated it to fetch all linked files using the newly created method.

The method createFileTooltip() was only picking the first file’s link.

updated it to iterate over all files and join them using newline characters

summary: "Add subgroup" does not work on first time
Fix: #12274 Import at "Find Unlinked Files" should not re-add an identical entry

description.text:
Closes #12274 
#### Summary
This fix prevents "Search for unlinked local files" from re-adding already imported files with a second click on "Import". All import files will be unchecked. This provides visual indication to the user that their import is finish.

#### Changes Made

- `startImport` in UnlinkedFilesDialog unchecks all files after importing
